title: Who Is Misato or Misatos Exclusive Halfway House
display_title: Who Is Misato or Misatos Exclusive Halfway House
wikitext:
[]: Who Is Misato or Misato's Exclusive Halfway House             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Fri Aug 13, 2010 3:44 am Lately, I've been trying to figure out Misato's character more and the more I mull it over in my head, the less sense she makes. Their seems to be only three people she actually interacts with Shinji, Kaji, and Ritsuko. Take her relationship with Asuka. What is her relationship with Asuka? She obviously has some attachment to the child, she had even been her guardian in germany for about a year or two(?). Yet she never seems to actually interact with Asuka in a meaningful way. When she's not simply ignoring Asuka, she's dismisses what she says. But even that relationship fits in with Misato's brand of self-serving compassion. The most bewildering of all is the scene where she is telling Ritsuko that Shinji will be moving in with her. During that scene, she casually mentions that Rei lives in a slum, but what is strange is her complete lack of concern for Rei, all the more jarring because it's a scene that exists to show that she is concerned for the pilot(s) under her. Now, it is unlikely that Gendo would let Rei move in with her, but still, you'd think the fact that one of her pilots is living in what can only be called the bombed-out set of Eraserhead is one of the few things she would not take lightly. In fact, it seemed like a significant characteristic of hers that the lives of the pilots was one of the few things she took seriously. What is her stance on Rei? Does she hate her? But hate would suggest an emotional response, whereas Misato exhibits a complete disregard for Rei. Is she simply not a huggable enough ball of autism? She seemed sad when Rei II, went kablooey? Wait, I can't remember if she was crying or simply had that shadow covering the eyes thing that happens in anime? I think Misato may have had the shadow-over-eyes thing, and Maya was the one crying. You know Maya, always going off the handle whenever blackops commandoes come to kill everyone or a child soldier is mindraped/selfdestructs while in the process of being mindraped by an eldritch abomination. tl;dr- What is Misato's Deal? What is her relationship with Asuka? What is her relationship with Rei? Why isn't Rei invited to live in Misato's Mighty Mead Hall along with the rest of Earth's Champions? What was the Deal of the psychological diary of Shinji she was keeping?

[]: Re: Who Is Misato or Misato's Exclusive Halfway House             Quote Post by carla » Fri Aug 13, 2010 9:23 am Sun Stealer wrote: Take her relationship with Asuka. What is her relationship with Asuka? She obviously has some attachment to the child, she had even been her guardian in germany for about a year or two(?). Yet she never seems to actually interact with Asuka in a meaningful way. When she's not simply ignoring Asuka, she's dismisses what she says.

[]: Re: Who Is Misato or Misato's Exclusive Halfway House             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Fri Aug 13, 2010 9:58 am carla wrote: i don't remember Rei ever being mentioned in that conversation. i also don't think her taking Shinji in had anything to do with the living conditions. AFAIR she only objected to Shinji's original assigned accomodations because he was going to be alone.

[]: Re: Who Is Misato or Misato's Exclusive Halfway House             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:20 am Sun Stealer wrote: Why isn't Rei invited to live in Misato's Mighty Mead Hall along with the rest of Earth's Champions?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:45 am I suppose that Gendo may have told Misato on what she should be doing with Rei, those do's and dont's that she must follow. Thus officially on paper, Misato is Rei's commanding officer, but the real authority controlling that girl is Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 13, 2010 12:15 pm Regarding Asuka, I think Carla has the relationship more or less nailed down. I would add, however, that part of the reason they keep each other at a distance is that both sees (if only subconsciously until Instrumentality) their Shadow in the other and neither really wants to have to deal with that. She probably isn't and never has been her guardian, more likely she's simply the assigned handler. I wonder if Asuka's family still has custody of her though, as she would probably easily qualify to become an emancipated minor which would be an obvious step to take in her quest to be treated as an adult. I'd like to think it's reasonable to assume that at some point Misato did complain about Rei's living conditions and was ordered to mind her own business. On the other hand, if he shot her down like that earlier why in the world would she think having Shinji live with her would be allowed? Very puzzling.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Aug 13, 2010 1:38 pm The slums aren't mentioned in the manga.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Aug 13, 2010 2:19 pm I can easily imagine that Misato asked Gendo about Rei's living conditions before, and Gendo pulled a "Stay out of her business or you're fucking fired" card.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Fri Aug 13, 2010 4:50 pm I could have sworn I heard that dialogue somewhere, but I could be wrong. Now I'm not even sure if it was the living accomodations scene, or if it was even Misato that said it. I know someone, somewhere commented on Rei's living conditions in the show. I have searchin' to do. You all make good points concerning Rei, but just one thing. One can be given orders, but that doesn't mean one has to like them. Gendo could order Misato to mind her own business, and Misato would have to obey, but that wouldn't mean she still wouldn't have concern, and that it wouldn't show; she just can't do anything about it and has to keep her mouth shut. Back to the issue of Asuka and Misato. Misato clearly held some affection for Asuka in some manner. She did take her in, after all. She was Asuka's handler for years, so it's not like she didn't know what she was getting into. But hell, you'd think Misato wouldn't want Asuka there. There relationship is like a marginally more relaxed version of what it would be like if Shinji lived with Gendo.

[]: Re: Who Is Misato or Misato's Exclusive Halfway House             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Aug 13, 2010 8:05 pm Sun Stealer wrote: tl;dr- What is Misato's Deal? What is her relationship with Asuka? What is her relationship with Rei? Why isn't Rei invited to live in Misato's Mighty Mead Hall along with the rest of Earth's Champions? What was the Deal of the psychological diary of Shinji she was keeping?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 13, 2010 10:15 pm Sun Stealer : Are you thinking of this? It sort of implies Rei's living conditions are crappy. Episode 05 wrote: RITSUKO: Oh, I almost forgot! Shinji, would you do me a favor? Shinji (OFF): What is it? RITSUKO: Rei Ayanami's renewal card. I keep forgetting to give this to her. Would you mind delivering it before you go to headquarters? Shinji: Sure. Misato: What's the matter? You're staring at Rei's picture. Shinji: Nothing! Misato: Shinji, don't tell me.. Shinji: No! Misato: Oh, come on! You're blushing! Misato: Well, you've got an official excuse to go to Rei's place now. This is your chance, isn't it? Shinji: Stop teasing me! Misato: But I love teasing you. You always take it so seriously! RITSUKO: Just like Misato. Shinji: I just find it strange that I know so little about Ayanami, even though we're both Eva pilots. RITSUKO: She's a very nice girl, but she's like your father. She's not very adept. Shinji: Not adept at what? RITSUKO: Living.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:01 pm How does that imply anything about her living conditions?

[]:             Quote Post by MugwumpHasNoLiver » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:09 pm It implies she's not very adept at them. Duh.

[]: Re: Who Is Misato or Misato's Exclusive Halfway House             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:39 pm child of Lilith wrote: lol I think Gendo probably told d her to leave it alone when it comes to anything involving Rei’s personal life. Also, Rei couldn’t care less where she lives so even if Misato had gone to her directly and invited her to come stay with her Rei would have just declined the offer.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:39 pm I've always found it rather ironic that Ritsko of all people would say this. sun stealer: just because she did nothing doesent mean she didn't give a damn at all. Misato had enough on her plate as is without playing part-time socialworker. Especially to someone who clearly wanted to be left alone.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Aug 13, 2010 11:57 pm MugwumpHasNoLiver wrote: It implies she's not very adept at them. Duh.

[]:             Quote Post by BC Baron » Sat Aug 14, 2010 1:50 am @Sun Stealer: FWIW, I seem to recall Touji making a brief remark about Rei's living conditions when he and Shinji go to visit her apartment in episode 17. Not sure whether that might have been the scene you were thinking of? Also, I think Misato probably tried to reach out to Rei via minor gestures in the early going, like by inviting her to attend the small party they throw (to celebrate Misato's promotion) in episode 12 or by including her in that little victory dinner at the ramen stand. Basically, I think she wanted to let Rei know that she was welcome to be a part of the group. My guess is that she eventually decided to dial back the whole 'be a friend/big sister thing' towards Rei for a few reasons: She wasn't really making much progress as far as getting Rei to view her as anything other than a commanding officer. Rei never seems to open up around Misato, keeping their relationship on a strictly formal level (I'm pretty sure Rei only ever addressed her as "Major Katsuragi"). She may have noticed how Rei was starting to come out of her shell to some minor degree, most likely due to her friendship with Shinji and perhaps told herself, "Okay, at least she's socializing with someone her own age". Ultimately, she realizes that for the purposes of piloting Eva, Rei is probably the most emotionally balanced Child they have on the roster and doesn't require as much attention as the others. I think she even comments to Ritsuko at some point about how they've never had any problems with Rei disobeying any of NERV's orders. Of course, depending on how much you want to psychoanalyze Misato's character, I suppose you could make the argument that she has a greater obstacle to overcome in dealing with her awkwardness around Rei compared to the other pilots. When you take into account that when Misato was about Rei's age, she had just witnessed Second Impact and, as a result, spent an extended period mute due to the emotional trauma, perhaps Rei's quiet, introverted behavior reminds her of a time she'd rather not think about.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:49 am Misato has no opinion on Rei and her living conditions because Rei has been in Nerv's hospital the entire time Misato has been in tokyo-3. Before that, Misato worked for Nerv internationally. Before that, she probably had no knowledge of Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:02 pm ^LOL WAT?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 14, 2010 4:31 pm slothen wrote: Misato has no opinion on Rei and her living conditions because Rei has been in Nerv's hospital the entire time Misato has been in tokyo-3. Before that, Misato worked for Nerv internationally. Before that, she probably had no knowledge of Rei.
